title: asperula tinctoria subsp. tinctoria
intro: Asperula tinctoria subsp. tinctoria, also known as red root or orange root, is a plant species belonging to the gentian family, native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, antispasmodic, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, wounds, and skin problems. The health benefits of Asperula tinctoria subsp. tinctoria include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. Asperula tinctoria subsp. tinctoria can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve to be used internally or externally, depending on the intended application.
